question: [File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Medium: Impacts caused by griefing with no economic damage to any user on the network'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the pinger to produce a misleading 'transfer success' log with a zero elapsed time by making `cryptoTransfer.Execute(client)` and `resp.GetReceipt(client)` both return instantly with cached/mocked responses, so the pinger reports sub-millisecond latency that does not reflect real network conditions?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Medium: Impacts caused by griefing with no economic damage to any user on the network'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the pinger to exhaust file descriptors by making `cryptoTransfer.Execute(client)` open new connections on every attempt without closing them, combined with the retry loop creating many failed connections that are not properly cleaned up?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Medium: Impacts caused by griefing with no economic damage to any user on the network'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the pinger to produce incorrect retry statistics by observing that the loop variable `i` starts at 1 and `attempts = maxRetries + 1`, so the log message 'attempt 1/11' on the first try is confusing and could cause operators to misconfigure `maxRetries`?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Medium: Impacts caused by griefing with no economic damage to any user on the network'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the pinger to block the main goroutine indefinitely by making `submitWithRetry` never return (e.g., by making both `cryptoTransfer.Execute` and `resp.GetReceipt` hang), preventing the ticker from processing any subsequent ticks?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Medium: Impacts caused by griefing with no economic damage to any user on the network'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the pinger to produce a false positive health signal by making `receipt.Status` return a value whose `String()` method returns 'SUCCESS' even for a non-success status code, exploiting any inconsistency in the SDK's status string mapping?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Medium: Impacts caused by griefing with no economic damage to any user on the network'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the pinger to waste CPU cycles by making `hiero.AccountIDFromString(cfg.toAccountID)` perform expensive string parsing on every tick invocation, since `toID` is re-parsed inside `submitWithRetry` rather than being cached at startup?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Medium: Impacts caused by griefing with no economic damage to any user on the network'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the pinger to produce misleading error messages by making `lastErr` contain a wrapped error from a previous attempt that is unrelated to the final failure, so `fmt.Errorf('all attempts failed: %w', lastErr)` reports the wrong root cause?
